jd_text:
Company Description
 Prowiden Overseas is a premier consultancy specializing in securing work visas for professionals in Germany, the UK, and across Europe, particularly in the hospitality industry. From chefs to bartenders and baristas, our services are tailored to facilitate seamless international career transitions. With expertise spanning UK Work Visas, Germany Work Visas, and Schengen Work Visas, we provide dedicated support for various roles, including specialized chef and hospitality work visas. Our team has successfully assisted countless individuals in navigating complex visa processes and achieving their dream of working abroad. Join us in connecting talent with thriving opportunities across Europe.
 Role Description
 Prowiden Overseas is seeking a full-time Head of Partnerships to lead its partnership initiatives. Based on-site in Dubai, this dynamic role involves identifying, building, and nurturing relationships with strategic partners across industries to expand the company's network and service offerings. The role includes developing and executing partnership strategies, negotiating agreements, collaborating with cross-functional teams, and analyzing partnerships' impact on business growth. You will act as a key ambassador of the company, fostering long-term alliances to drive success.
 Qualifications
 Strong expertise in Business Relationship Management and building high-value partnerships.Experience in Strategic Partnerships and developing growth-focused partnership strategies.Proven Analytical Skills for assessing partnership performance and identifying opportunities.Outstanding Communication skills to engage effectively with stakeholders and team members.Proficiency in negotiation and the ability to manage cross-functional collaboration effectively.Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field; MBA is an advantage.Prior experience in leadership roles related to partnerships is highly desirable.Familiarity with the hospitality industry or global talent mobility is a bonus.
